mechanism: Induces oxidative/proteotoxic stress and apoptosis (affects redox/proteostasis pathways)
additional_notes: Repurposing candidate with reported AML cytotoxicity in preclinical studies. No explicit concentration range provided in the text.

mechanism: Copper-dependent proteotoxic effects and proteasome inhibition leading to cancer cell death
additional_notes: Often evaluated together with copper; marketed as Antabuse. Reported to kill various cancer cells including AML models. No explicit concentration range provided in the text.

mechanism: Reported inhibition of STAT3 and Wnt signaling and anti-proliferative effects in AML preclinical studies
additional_notes: Repurposing candidate with reported activity in AML models. No explicit concentration range provided in the text.

mechanism: Alters cellular metabolism (complex I inhibition) and can have antiproliferative/chemo-sensitizing effects
additional_notes: Frequently investigated in combinations in AML models. No explicit concentration range provided in the text.

mechanism: Inhibits autophagy/lysosomal function to overcome cytoprotective autophagy
additional_notes: Text also mentions chloroquine as a related compound; both investigated in AML combinations. No explicit concentration range provided in the text.

mechanism: Inhibit cholesterol biosynthesis pathway (HMG-CoA reductase) and reported to induce apoptosis / synergize with AML agents
additional_notes: Examples given: simvastatin, lovastatin. Class-level repurposing candidates; no explicit concentration range provided in the text.

mechanism: Inhibits the 26S proteasome, disrupting proteostasis and inducing apoptosis
additional_notes: Marketed as Velcade; used in myeloma and shown activity/synergy in AML preclinical models and trials. No explicit concentration range provided in the text.

mechanism: Inhibits Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK); may have off-target effects relevant to some AML contexts
additional_notes: Marketed as Imbruvica; investigated for activity in leukemic stem cell contexts and subsets of AML. No explicit concentration range provided in the text.

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1/2 signaling to modulate cytokine signaling and cell proliferation
additional_notes: Marketed as Jakafi; explored in AML and post-MPN AML settings and combinations. No explicit concentration range provided in the text.

mechanism: Induces oxidative stress and proteolytic/degradation pathways; established in APL and explored in other AML contexts
additional_notes: Marketed as Trisenox; mechanistic activity noted for non-APL AML exploration. No explicit concentration range provided in the text.

mechanism: Antagonizes CXCR4 to disrupt leukemia–niche interactions and sensitize blasts to therapy
additional_notes: Also known as AMD3100; used clinically for stem-cell mobilization. No explicit concentration range provided in the text.

mechanism: MAO inhibitor that also inhibits LSD1 (KDM1A) leading to differentiation effects in AML
additional_notes: Text mentions tranylcypromine and derivatives as LSD1 inhibitors; differentiation strategy under clinical investigation. No explicit concentration range provided in the text.

mechanism: Inhibits SRC/ABL and other kinases to reduce proliferation in some AML subtypes
additional_notes: Marketed as Sprycel; noted to have effects in some AML cell lines. No explicit concentration range provided in the text.

category: NAD metabolism modulators / NAMPT pathway agents
mechanism: Target NAD metabolism (e.g., NAMPT inhibition) to exploit metabolic vulnerabilities in AML
